questions.originalStem: 高速自動車国道で自動二輪車の二人乗りができるのは、自動二輪車の運転経験３年以上、年齢が２０歳以上でなければならないが、自動二輪車の運転経験が１年以上あれば自動車専用道路でのみ二人乗りをすることができる。
questions.englishStem: To be able to ride two-seaters on a motorcycle on national expressways, a person must have at least three years of experience driving a motorcycle and be at least 20 years old, but if you have at least one year of experience driving a motorcycle, you can only ride two-seaters on expressways.

questions.originalStem: 車が他の車を追い越すとき、前車の左側に十分な間隔があれば、左側から追い越してもよい。
questions.englishStem: When passing another vehicle, a vehicle may pass from the left side if there is sufficient space on the left side of the vehicle in front.

questions.originalStem: 出発地警察署長の許可があれば、荷台や座席以外の場所に荷物を積んだり、荷台に人を乗せて運転することができる。
questions.englishStem: If you have permission from the police chief at the place of departure, you can load luggage in places other than the cargo bed or seats, and you can drive with people on the cargo bed.

questions.originalStem: 自動車の運転は、認知、判断、操作に分けられ、このうちとくに大切なのは操作で、交通事故の大半は操作の誤りによって起きている。
questions.englishStem: Driving a car can be divided into recognition, judgment, and operation, and among these, operation is particularly important, and the majority of traffic accidents occur due to operational errors.

questions.originalStem: 高速道路では、登坂車線も本線車道に含まれる。
questions.englishStem: On expressways, uphill lanes are also included in the main roadway.

questions.originalStem: 徐行しようとするときと、停止しようとするときの手の合図の方法は同じである。
questions.englishStem: The hand signals used when slowing down and when stopping are the same.

questions.originalStem: 普通免許や二輪免許、原付免許を取得して１年以内に交通違反などを犯し、違反点数の合計が３点以上になったとき（１回の違反で３点に達したときを除く）は初心運転者講習を受けることができる。
questions.englishStem: If you commit a traffic violation within one year of obtaining a regular driver's license, motorcycle license, or moped license, and the total number of violation points is 3 or more (excluding cases where you have 3 points for a single violation), you can take a beginner driver's course.

questions.originalStem: ブレーキのききが悪かったので、交通量の少ない道路を選んで運転した。
questions.englishStem: The brakes were not working well, so I chose a road with less traffic to drive.

questions.originalStem: 乗車定員１０人の車は、中型免許以上がないと運転できない。
questions.englishStem: A car with a seating capacity of 10 people cannot be driven unless you have a medium-duty license or higher.

questions.originalStem: 二輪車でブレーキをかけるときは、前輪ブレーキと後輪ブレーキを同時にかけるのがよい。
questions.englishStem: When applying the brakes on a two-wheeled vehicle, it is best to apply the front and rear brakes at the same time.

questions.originalStem: 一般道路で法定最高速度が６０キロメートル毎時の車はすべて、高速自動車国道の本線車道を１００キロメートル毎時で走行できる。
questions.englishStem: All vehicles with a legal maximum speed of 60 km/h on ordinary roads can travel at 100 km/h on the main lanes of national highways.

questions.originalStem: 近くに交差点のない道路で緊急自動車に進路をゆずるときは、道路の左側に寄って進路をゆずればよい（一方通行路を除く）。
questions.englishStem: When yielding to an emergency vehicle on a road with no nearby intersections, simply move to the left side of the road and yield to the emergency vehicle (excluding one-way streets).

questions.originalStem: 正面の信号が「赤色の灯火」と「黄色の灯火の矢印」を示しているとき、自動車は黄色の矢印の方向に進んでもよい。
questions.englishStem: When the traffic light in front of you shows a "red light" and a "yellow light arrow", the vehicle may proceed in the direction of the yellow arrow.

questions.originalStem: 高速道路で故障などのためやむを得ず駐車する場合は、車の後方の路上に停止表示機材を置き、後続車の運転者にわかるようにしなければならないが、歩行が困難な場合は停止表示灯を車の側方の路上に置いてもよい。
questions.englishStem: If you are forced to park on a highway due to a breakdown, etc., you must place a stop indicator on the road behind your car so that drivers of following cars can see it, but if you have difficulty walking, you may place a stop indicator light on the road to the side of your car.

questions.originalStem: 一般道路での大型貨物自動車の最高速度は、５０キロメートル毎時である。
questions.englishStem: The maximum speed for large lorries on public roads is 50 kilometers per hour.

questions.originalStem: この３つの補助標識は、同じ意味を表わしている。
questions.englishStem: These three auxiliary indicators express the same meaning.

questions.originalStem: 舗装道路では、雨の降り始めのほうが、降っている最中より、スリップしやすい。
questions.englishStem: Paved roads are more prone to slipping at the beginning of rain than during the rain.

questions.originalStem: 交差点で対面する信号が青色の場合は、車はすべて直進し、左折し、右折することができる。
questions.englishStem: If the traffic light facing you at an intersection is green, all cars can go straight, turn left, and turn right.

questions.originalStem: 遠心力は、およそ速度の２乗に比例して大きくなり、カーブの半径が小さいほど大きくなる。
questions.englishStem: Centrifugal force increases approximately in proportion to the square of the speed, and increases as the radius of the curve becomes smaller.

questions.originalStem: この標示の路側帯は、歩行者の通行を妨げなければ、自転車も通行することができる。
questions.englishStem: Bicycles can also pass along the roadside strips marked with this sign as long as they do not obstruct pedestrian traffic.

questions.originalStem: 高速自動車国道の本線車道が、道路の構造上往復の方向別に分離されていない区間では、普通自動車の最高速度は、６０キロメートル毎時である。
questions.englishStem: In sections where the main lanes of national expressways are not separated by direction due to road structure, the maximum speed for ordinary vehicles is 60 kilometers per hour.

questions.originalStem: 大型特殊免許で運転できるのは、大型自動車、中型自動車、大型特殊自動車、小型特殊自動車、一般原動機付自転車である。
questions.englishStem: A large-sized special license allows you to drive large cars, medium-sized cars, large special cars, small special cars, and regular motorized bicycles.

questions.originalStem: 普通免許を受けていれば、１．５トン積みのトラックを運転することができる。
questions.englishStem: If you have a regular license, you can drive a 1.5 ton truck.

questions.originalStem: 自動二輪車は、自動二輪免許を受けていても、免許を受けていた期間が１年未満の者は、二人乗りをしてはいけない。
questions.englishStem: Even if you have a motorcycle license, if you have been licensed for less than one year, you may not ride a motorcycle with two people.

questions.originalStem: 同一方向に二つの車両通行帯がある道路では、速度の遅い車は左側の通行帯を、速度の速い車は右側の通行帯を通行する。
questions.englishStem: On a road with two lanes running in the same direction, slower vehicles use the left lane, and faster vehicles use the right lane.

questions.originalStem: この標識のある通行帯を午後８時に普通自動車で通行した。
questions.englishStem: I drove a regular car through the lane marked with this sign at 8 p.m.
questions.imageAltTextEn: Illustration for honte-03-42.

questions.originalStem: 路線バス等優先通行帯を普通自動車で通行中、通園バスが後方に接近してきたが、路線バスではないので、そのまま通行した。
questions.englishStem: While I was driving a regular car in the priority lane for route buses, a school bus approached behind me, but since it was not a route bus, I continued on my way.

questions.originalStem: 道路に面した場所に出入りするため歩道や路側帯を横切るときは、徐行して歩行者の通行を妨げないようにしなければならない。
questions.englishStem: When crossing a sidewalk or roadside strip to enter or exit an area facing the road, you must drive slowly so as not to obstruct pedestrian traffic.

questions.originalStem: 制動距離は、空走距離と停止距離を合わせたものである。
questions.englishStem: Braking distance is the sum of the free running distance and the stopping distance.

questions.originalStem: こう配の急な上り坂は、徐行すべき場所にはなっていない。
questions.englishStem: A steep uphill slope is not a place where you should drive slowly.

questions.originalStem: タイヤと路面の摩擦は、空走距離と制動距離に大きな関係がある。
questions.englishStem: The friction between the tires and the road surface has a large relationship with the free running distance and braking distance.

questions.originalStem: オートマチック車は、チェンジレバーがＰ（パーキング）やＮ（ニュートラル）以外の位置ではエンジンがかからない。
questions.englishStem: In automatic cars, the engine will not start if the change lever is in any position other than P (park) or N (neutral).

questions.originalStem: 自動二輪車や一般原動機付自転車の荷台には、どちらも６０キログラムまで荷物を積むことができる。
questions.englishStem: Both motorcycles and motorized bicycles can carry up to 60 kilograms of luggage.

questions.originalStem: サイドミラーよりルームミラーのほうが後続車との距離を正しく目測することができる。
questions.englishStem: The rearview mirror allows you to visually estimate the distance to the vehicle behind you more accurately than the side mirror.

questions.originalStem: 貨物自動車で高速走行するときは、車の積み荷の重心が高い方が安定した走行ができる 。
questions.englishStem: When traveling at high speeds with a freight vehicle, the higher the center of gravity of the vehicle's cargo, the more stable the vehicle will be.

questions.originalStem: 二輪車でカーブをまがる時は、遠心力に対抗するため車体を外側に傾けてまがるようにする 。
questions.englishStem: When turning a curve on a two-wheeled vehicle, tilt the body outward to counter centrifugal force.

questions.originalStem: トラックの荷台に荷物の見張りのため人を１人だけ乗せる場合は警察署長の許可を受けずに乗せることができる 。
questions.englishStem: If only one person is to be placed on the bed of a truck to guard the cargo, they may do so without obtaining permission from the police chief.

questions.originalStem: 交通事故が起きた場合は、続発事故を防ぐため、他の交通の妨げにならないような安全な場所に車を移動させ、エンジンを切っておくようにする 。
questions.englishStem: If a traffic accident occurs, to prevent further accidents, move your vehicle to a safe location where it will not interfere with other traffic, and turn off the engine.

questions.originalStem: ４００ccの自動二輪車に荷物を積む場合の高さ制限は地上２メートル以下とされている 。
questions.englishStem: The height restriction for loading luggage onto a 400cc motorcycle is 2 meters or less above the ground.

questions.originalStem: 故障車をロープでけん引する場合は、ロープの長さは５メートル以内で、ロープの中央に０．３メートル平方以上の赤布をつけなければならない 。
questions.englishStem: When towing a broken-down vehicle with a rope, the length of the rope must be less than 5 meters, and a red cloth of at least 0.3 meters square must be attached to the center of the rope.

questions.originalStem: 運転者は同乗者が不用意にドアを開けたりしないように注意する義務がある 。
questions.englishStem: The driver has a duty to be careful to prevent passengers from opening the door carelessly.

questions.originalStem: 乗車定員１０人の自動車は普通免許で運転することができる 。
questions.englishStem: A car with a seating capacity of 10 people can be driven with a regular driver's license.

questions.originalStem: 検査標章の色と数字は検査を受けた年月を表す 。
questions.englishStem: The color and number of the inspection mark indicate the month and year of inspection.

questions.originalStem: 図の路側帯の設けられている道路で駐車する場合、左側に０．７５メートル以上の余地を空けることができれば、路側帯の中へ入って駐車することができる 。
questions.englishStem: When parking on a road with a roadside strip as shown in the figure, if you can leave at least 0.75 meters of space on the left side, you can park inside the roadside strip.

questions.originalStem: オートマチック車がＤレンジで走行中、何らかの原因でエンジンが止まってしまった場合は一旦Ｎレンジへ戻さないとエンジンスイッチが作動しない 。
questions.englishStem: If an automatic car is running in D range and the engine stops for some reason, the engine switch will not operate unless you return it to N range.

questions.originalStem: ６６０cc以下の普通自動車の高速自動車国道での最高速度は８０キロメートル毎時である 。
questions.englishStem: The maximum speed for regular cars of 660 cc or less on national highways is 80 km/h.

questions.originalStem: カーブを走行する時、速度が２倍になると遠心力は４倍になる 。
questions.englishStem: When driving around a curve, if the speed doubles, the centrifugal force quadruples.

questions.originalStem: 高速道路で故障して停止する場合、夜間は停止表示機材を置かなければならないが、昼間は非常点滅表示灯をつければ停止表示機材は置かなくてもよい 。
questions.englishStem: If a vehicle breaks down on an expressway and has to stop, it is necessary to put up a stop sign at night, but if a flashing emergency indicator light is turned on during the day, there is no need to put up a stop sign.

questions.originalStem: 乗車定員５人の乗用車には、運転者の他に１２歳の子供６人を乗せることができる 。
questions.englishStem: In addition to the driver, six 12-year-old children can also be carried in a five-person passenger car.

questions.originalStem: 普通免許を取得して１年未満の者が普通自動車を運転する場合は、車の前後に「初心者マーク」（初心運転者標識）を付けなければならない 。
questions.englishStem: If a person who has had a regular driver's license for less than a year is driving a regular car, a ``beginner mark'' (beginner driver sign) must be placed on the front and back of the vehicle.

questions.originalStem: 交通事故が発生した場合、人身事故となった場合は必ず警察官への届出が必要であるが、物損事故の場合は届出の必要はない 。
questions.englishStem: When a traffic accident occurs, it is necessary to report it to a police officer if it results in personal injury, but there is no need to report it if the accident results in property damage.

questions.originalStem: タイヤの空気圧が低い状態で高速走行を続けると、スタンディングウエーブ現象が起きる危険性がある 。
questions.englishStem: If you continue to drive at high speed with low tire pressure, there is a risk of standing wave phenomenon.

questions.originalStem: 高速自動車国道や自動車専用道路での自動二輪車の二人乗りは自動二輪車の運転経験が１年以上あれば行うことができる 。
questions.englishStem: Riding two-wheeled motorcycles on national expressways and expressways is only possible if you have at least one year of motorcycle driving experience.

questions.originalStem: 交通事故の責任は事故を起こした運転者のみにあるので、車を貸した者には責任がない 。
questions.englishStem: The responsibility for a traffic accident lies only with the driver who caused the accident, so the person who lent the car is not responsible.

questions.originalStem: 駐車禁止の標識は「規制標識」であり、停止線や中央線は「指示標示」である 。
questions.englishStem: No-parking signs are "regulatory signs," and stop lines and center lines are "instruction signs."

questions.originalStem: 荷物が軽い時に比べて重い時の方がタイヤの接地抵抗が増すので荷物が重い時の方が制動距離は短くなる 。
questions.englishStem: The ground resistance of the tires increases when the load is heavy compared to when the load is light, so the braking distance becomes shorter when the load is heavy.

questions.originalStem: ハンドルやブレーキが故障している車は、徐行してでも運転してはならない 。
questions.englishStem: Do not drive a car with a malfunctioning steering wheel or brakes, even if you drive slowly.

questions.originalStem: 道路を自動車の保管場所として使用してはならない 。
questions.englishStem: Roads may not be used as storage areas for motor vehicles.

questions.originalStem: 車から離れる時は、ほんの少しの間でもエンジンを止めてキーを抜き、ドアロックをして盗難防止の措置をしなければならない 。
questions.englishStem: When leaving your car, even if only for a moment, you must stop the engine, remove the key, and lock the doors to prevent theft.

questions.originalStem: エンジンのかかっていない一般原動機付自転車や、自動二輪車を押して歩くときは歩行者として扱われる 。
questions.englishStem: If you are walking while pushing a regular motorized bicycle or motorcycle that does not have an engine running, you will be treated as a pedestrian.

questions.originalStem: オートマチック車のエンジンブレーキは効果がないので、下り坂などでは、ブレーキペダルとハンドブレーキで減速するのがよい 。
questions.englishStem: Engine braking in automatic cars is ineffective, so when going downhill, it's best to use the brake pedal and handbrake to slow down.

questions.originalStem: ７０歳以上の運転者が普通自動車を運転するときは車の前後に「高齢運転者標識」をつけるのが望ましい 。
questions.englishStem: When drivers over the age of 70 drive regular cars, it is recommended that they display ``elderly driver signs'' on the front and rear of the vehicle.

questions.originalStem: 高齢運転者標識や身体障害者標識、初心運転者標識をつけて走行している自動車に対して、危険を避けるためやむを得ない場合の他は、側方に幅よせしたり、前方に割り込んだりしてはならない 。
questions.englishStem: Unless it is unavoidable to avoid danger, do not swerve to the side or cut in front of a vehicle that is running with an elderly driver's sign, a disabled person's sign, or a novice driver's sign.

questions.originalStem: 一方通行の道路に駐車するときは、道路の側端に沿って止めれば、左右どちら側に駐車してもよい 。
questions.englishStem: When parking on a one-way road, you can park on either side as long as you park along the edge of the road.

questions.originalStem: 図の標識のある道路では、道路の右側にはみ出さなければ追い越しをすることができる 。
questions.englishStem: On roads with the signs shown in the figure, you can overtake as long as you do not go to the right side of the road.

questions.originalStem: 交通が渋滞しているときは、横断歩道や自転車横断帯の中に入って停止してもやむを得ない 。
questions.englishStem: When traffic is congested, it is unavoidable to stop at a crosswalk or bicycle crossing zone.

questions.originalStem: 車の下から冷却水やオイルが漏れているときは故障が考えられるので運転してはならないが、空調装置からの排水は異常ではない 。
questions.englishStem: Do not drive if coolant or oil is leaking from under the car as it may be malfunctioning, but drainage from the air conditioner is not abnormal.

questions.originalStem: 高速道路の登坂車線は重い荷物を積んだ貨物自動車が走行するためのものなので、普通乗用自動車で登坂車線を通行することはできない 。
questions.englishStem: The uphill lanes on expressways are for freight vehicles carrying heavy loads, so regular passenger cars cannot use them.

questions.originalStem: オートマチック車のクリープ現象とは、チェンジレバーがＤレンジに入っている状態でアクセルを踏まなくても動き出す現象をいう 。
questions.englishStem: The creep phenomenon in automatic cars refers to the phenomenon in which the car starts moving without pressing the accelerator when the change lever is in D range.

questions.originalStem: 前照燈が故障して点灯しなかったが、昼間だけ運転するのであれば、そのまま運転してかまわない 。
questions.englishStem: The headlights were malfunctioning and wouldn't turn on, but if you only drive during the day, you can continue to drive.

questions.originalStem: 時速６０キロメートル毎時の速度で衝突したときは、ビルの５階（約１４メートル）から落ちた場合と同程度の衝撃力を受けることになる 。
questions.englishStem: In a collision at a speed of 60 kilometers per hour, the force of impact would be equivalent to that of falling from the fifth floor (approximately 14 meters) of a building.

questions.originalStem: 故障車をロープでけん引する場合はけん引免許を持っていなくても運転することができる 。
questions.englishStem: If you want to tow a broken down vehicle with a rope, you can do so even if you do not have a towing license.

questions.originalStem: タイヤの空気圧は徐々に減少することがあるので、日常点検等で確認することが大切である 。
questions.englishStem: Tire air pressure may gradually decrease, so it is important to check it during daily inspections.

questions.originalStem: 図の標識がある交差点を一般原動機付自転車で右折する場合は自動車と同じ方法で右折する 。
questions.englishStem: When turning right on an ordinary motorized bicycle at an intersection with the sign shown in the figure, turn right in the same way as a car.

questions.originalStem: 駐車禁止の場所であっても、運転者が運転席に乗車した状態であれば５分以内に限り友人を待つため停車することができる 。
questions.englishStem: Even in areas where parking is prohibited, if the driver is in the driver's seat, he or she may stop to wait for a friend for up to five minutes.

questions.originalStem: 往復の方向別に分離されている高速自動車国道での最低速度は５０キロメートル毎時である 。
questions.englishStem: The minimum speed on national expressways, which are separated by direction for round trips, is 50 kilometers per hour.

questions.originalStem: 総排気量１２５ccの自動二輪車は高速自動車国道だけでなく、自動車専用道路も通行することができない 。
questions.englishStem: Motorcycles with a total displacement of 125 cc cannot be used not only on national expressways but also on expressways.

questions.originalStem: こう配の急な坂道では駐車してはならない 。
questions.englishStem: Do not park on steep slopes.

questions.originalStem: 図の二種類の標識は、普通自動車の運転者に対しては同じ意味となる 。
questions.englishStem: The two types of signs shown in the diagram have the same meaning for drivers of ordinary vehicles.

questions.originalStem: 運転することが明らかな人に飲酒させた場合は、酒をすすめた人も処罰を受けることがある 。
questions.englishStem: If a person who is clearly driving is given alcohol, the person who provided the drink may also be subject to punishment.

questions.originalStem: 消火栓から５メートル以内の場所では駐停車が禁止されている 。
questions.englishStem: Parking is prohibited within 5 meters of a fire hydrant.

questions.originalStem: ＡＢＳ（アンチロック・ブレーキシステム）のついている車で急ブレーキをかけた場合にブレーキペダルが小刻みに振動するのは異常ではない 。
questions.englishStem: It is normal for the brake pedal to vibrate slightly when you brake suddenly in a car equipped with ABS (anti-lock braking system).

questions.originalStem: パンクしたタイヤを交換する時は、車輪をジャッキアップする前に車輪を締め付けているナットを少しゆるめておく 。
questions.englishStem: When replacing a flat tire, slightly loosen the nuts that tighten the wheel before jacking it up.

questions.originalStem: 交差点を走行中、緊急自動車が接近してきたので、直ちに交差点内で停止した 。
questions.englishStem: While driving through the intersection, an emergency vehicle approached, so I immediately stopped inside the intersection.

choices.englishText: A blue circle with a white arrow
A red circle with a white horizontal bar
I do not start to cross.
I will wait until the lights turn green.
I will press the button and wait. After the lights turn green, I will cross the road.
I will cross at a nearby pedestrian overpass.
Crossing is allowed if there are no moving vehicles around.
Only pedestrians are permitted to use the sidewalk.
They must stay inside the bicycle lane.
This sign is for motor vehicles only. Bicyclists do not need to stop here.
It is permitted, because it is unavoidable.
Using a phone when riding is not permitted.
Being familiar with the directions, leave with the light out.
With no spare batteries, leave the bicycle at home and walk.
Like a bicycle with a baby seat, you may carry your child as long as she is wearing a helmet.
Riding double is prohibited. You may not carry your child on your motorized bicycle.
The traffic light has turned to the green arrow (pointing right), so it is okay to turn right.
You may only turn right by making a hook turn.
We can turn left if there are no pedestrians or moving vehicles.
Turning left is prohibited when the light is red.
Wait until the cars in front move along because the zebra zone is closed to traffic.
Go through the zebra zone, paying attention to the movement of vehicles behind and ahead.
It is not okay to continue driving in the passing lane.
It is okay to continue driving in the passing lane.
Will not make a U-turn because U-turns are prohibited.
Will make a quick U-turn while paying close attention to the surroundings.

explanations.bodyEn: This statement is true. At an uncontrolled intersection, the basic rule is to yield to traffic approaching from your right unless a sign, signal, or road marking changes that priority.

This is false. Stopping or parking close to a crosswalk reduces visibility and endangers pedestrians, so the law restricts stopping within 5 meters before a crosswalk unless a specific exception applies.
This is true. At a railroad crossing in Japan, you are required to stop just before the tracks and confirm safety, even when the warning device is silent and the gate is open.
The correct answer is the red circular sign with a white horizontal bar. It means vehicles must not enter from that direction.
This is false. Entering the intersection first does not let you cut off an oncoming vehicle that is continuing straight. When you turn right, you must not obstruct that vehicle.
This is true. A stop sign requires a full stop at the stop line, or just before the intersection if no stop line is present.
This is true. Before a level crossing, stop before the crossing or stop line and confirm safety to both sides. If traffic lights control the crossing, follow those lights.
This is false. The driver must make sure passengers in both the front and rear seats wear seat belts, not only the people in front.
This is false. Street parking can still be a violation even for a short time. If you leave the car and cannot drive it away immediately, it may count as abandoned parking.
This is true. If front-end parking is designated, you must follow that instruction. In residential areas it helps reduce exhaust and noise toward nearby homes.
This is false. Accessible parking spaces are reserved for drivers with physical disabilities and must not be used by regular vehicles, even briefly.
This is true. Without a posted limit, regular automobiles must follow the default limit of 60 km/h on ordinary roads.
This is true. The expressway passing lane is for overtaking. After passing, you should move back into the driving lane instead of staying in the passing lane.
This is true. Drivers are expected to yield to pedestrians, so pedestrian safety takes priority in everyday traffic situations.
The sign shown is the no entry sign. It tells drivers that vehicles must not enter from that direction.
When the green light is flashing, pedestrians must not start crossing the road. Pedestrians already in the crosswalk must quickly finish crossing the road or turn around and return.

Where there are traffic lights with pedestrian crossing buttons, press the button and wait. Start to cross the road after the lights turn green. (After pressing the button, the lights may not immediately turn green.)

You must not cross roads where there are signs prohibiting pedestrians crossing. In addition, it is very dangerous to cross roads with guardrails. When walking in a crosswalk, keep away from the bicycle crossing lane.
This is a road sign permitting bicyclists to use the sidewalk with pedestrians. However, bicyclists must ride slowly on the roadway side of the sidewalk.

Bicycles are vehicles. They must, in principle, be ridden on the roadway. Bicyclists should ride on the left of the roadway, inside the lines marking pedestrian paths on the left , on the roadway side of sidewalks where bicycles are permitted, on bicycle paths, and in other permitted locations.
Bicycles are vehicles, so bicyclists must come to a stop at stop signs and check that it is safe before proceeding.

Making phone calls, e-mailing, texting and using headphones to listen to music while riding are all violations of the safety rules. These actions impede your ability to identify the dangers around you and may result in an accident.

You must not ride a bicycle with no headlight. Just because you always go there and know the area well, don't let your guard down. Riding with no headlight is extremely dangerous because it delays cars and pedestrians from noticing you (on your bicycle). Always keep your light on when riding at night to let others know that you are there.

This would be considered riding double. You may not carry your child on your motorized bicycle. Motorized bicycles of 50cc or less (category one) can only seat one person by law. Riding double is prohibited. For this reason, regardless of whether the person is an adult or a child, you may not take any passengers, even if they are wearing a helmet.

You must make a hook turn in accordance with the road sign. Because there is a blue regulatory sign just before the intersection with directions for motorized bicycles making a right turn (hook turn), you must follow those directions regardless of the number of lanes. Even if there is no regulatory sign, if there are three or more lanes (each side or one-way), you are required to make a hook turn. However, if there is a regulatory sign at the intersection prohibiting hook turns, you need to make a small turn.

In Japan, no car is in principle allowed to turn left when a traffic light is red. You also cannot turn right when the light is red. However, you can move in the direction indicated with a green arrow even if there is also a red light.

When the yellow arrow traffic signal is on, vehicles and pedestrians may not proceed. The yellow arrow traffic signal applies to streetcars, so only streetcars may turn right when this is on. For cars, this is the same as a red light, so they may not go in any direction.

You may drive through the zebra zone. The zebra zone is a guidance zone for safe and smooth driving and is not closed to traffic. However, its purpose is not for driving on, so it is inadvisable to actively use.

After overtaking another vehicle, you may not continue driving in the passing lane without returning to the driving lane. Once you have overtaken the vehicle if you can return to your original lane but you do not and you stay in the passing lane, you are committing a traffic zone violation. After you have finished passing, you must return to the driving lane.

You may not make a U-turn here because the road markings prohibit it. Similarly, do not make a U-turn if there is a No U-turn sign. In other locations too, if there is any danger of obstructing pedestrian or other vehicle traffic, U-turns are not allowed.
This is a lane for slow-moving vehicles. It is not necessary to always use the uphill lane.
This sign indicates "Pedestrian-only road".
If it is unavoidable, you can use a horn.
Do not make small talk with passengers.
This sign says "Width reduced".
If you cannot close the door while driving, you cannot continue driving.
If you see a passenger sticking their hand out the window, you must warn them and stop them.
A wear indicator checks the ``tread'' of a tire.
Business vehicles are inspected every three months.
Even at night, if you can clearly see from 50 meters behind you due to road lighting, there is no need to turn on lights.
Please do not obstruct the passage of emergency vehicles.
Parking or parking is prohibited on these two roadside strips.
If one side of the road has a cliff that can cause falls, and it is not possible to cross the road safely, vehicles on the side of the cliff must stop.
Parking is prohibited at this sign. Parking is not prohibited.
The legal maximum speed for large freight vehicles on the main lanes of national highways is 90 kilometers per hour.
You can also drive small special vehicles with a regular license.
Passing must be done on the right unless there is a vehicle turning right.
You do not need a towing license to tow a broken down vehicle.
This sign indicates "No Trespassing".
Remember to use this method in case of an emergency.

The loading limit for vehicles with a total displacement of 660cc or less is 1.1 times the length of the vehicle, width up to the width of the vehicle, and height of no more than 2.5 meters from the ground.
When parking on the street, you cannot park for more than 12 hours.
Motorcycles (including motorized bicycles) are prohibited in areas marked with this sign.
Do not apply sudden brakes when your tire is flat.
Before driving on the highway, you must check the levels of fuel, coolant, engine oil, etc.
Please drive slowly when passing by the school bus.
When there is a safe distance, you can pass at a slow speed.
If you are on a one-way road and moving to the left would actually obstruct your path, move to the right and yield.
Light vehicles and motorized bicycles making a two-step right turn cannot proceed at this traffic light.
This auxiliary sign indicates the end of traffic restrictions.
Only authorized vehicles, including those with roadside garages, are allowed to pass.
Parking or parking is prohibited within 5 meters in front of or behind a crosswalk or bicycle crossing zone.
Do not change course if you anticipate that the vehicle behind you will brake or turn suddenly to avoid you.
Parking or parking within 10 meters of a bus stop is prohibited.
Visibility is poorer at night, so you're more likely to get distracted than during the day due to distractions and lights.
You must always carry the original copy of your automobile liability insurance policy.
To avoid rolling backwards on a slope, press the brake pedal instead of the gas pedal.
This is an emergency measure on expressways. The same method can be used if you are stuck at a railroad crossing.
It is said that there is no need to use it when transporting people in case of sudden illness, or when traveling by bus or taxi.
Talking on a cell phone while driving is prohibited, regardless of road conditions.
Leave a safe distance or drive slowly, regardless of whether there is a roadside belt or not.
Stopping distances will be longer when driving on wet or icy roads, or when tires are worn down.
If there is an oncoming vehicle, a pedestrian may suddenly become invisible within the light of both lights. Make sure there are no pedestrians.
Just pull over to the left side of the road (no need to stop)
It is dangerous to slow down just before or during a curve. Slow down enough on the straight section in front of you.
Even if the main road is congested, only emergency vehicles are allowed to use the roadside strip.
When loading luggage, etc., you must be careful not to cover the turn signals, etc.
At night, pay attention to changes in the brightness of the rear lights of the vehicle in front of you.
The hand signal for backing up is to extend your arms diagonally, palms facing back, and move your arms back and forth.
When crossing a sidewalk or roadside, you must always stop immediately before crossing.
When driving on the road, it is important to put yourself in the other person's shoes and drive with consideration.
It may be difficult to see the person due to the lights from your car and oncoming cars. Make sure there are no people around before proceeding.
On one-way roads, the right side of the center of the vehicle is also allowed to pass.
During daily inspections, we check that the amount of brake oil and coolant in the reservoir tank is within the specified range.
No unauthorized vehicles are allowed on pedestrian roads.
Do not overtake cars or motorized bicycles in areas where overtaking is prohibited.
When the signal at a railroad crossing is blue, you can pass through it without stopping.
The greater the amount of centrifugal force that acts on a vehicle, the heavier it is loaded.
Parking and parking are prohibited on steep slopes, both up and down.
You may not park in a prohibited area, even if there is more than 3.5 meters of space on the right side of your vehicle.
When a police officer has his arms raised vertically, parallel traffic is the same as a yellow light.
Even if you are driving at a slow speed, do not cut in front of a regular vehicle displaying a temporary license practice sign.
This sign is a "bicycle crossing zone".
Only authorized vehicles are allowed to pass, but you must drive slowly when doing so.
If you can quickly get out of another lane when the bus approaches, you can pass, but if you can't get out, you can't pass.
When transporting dangerous goods, they must be securely packaged and loaded, and a sign board must be affixed to indicate that dangerous goods are being transported.
Even if you are driving on the right-of-way, cars that try to enter from the intersecting road will not always stop, so you must drive with full attention to the cars that are trying to enter.
If there is sufficient distance, you must stop just before the intersection.
This does not constitute driving without a license, but it does constitute a violation of not carrying a license.
It represents the center line and is not necessarily the center of the road.
On roads without sidewalks or roadside strips, park along the left edge of the road.
Traffic accidents can also occur due to illegal parking. Please refrain from illegally parking so as not to disturb other traffic.
Safe speed is a speed that takes into consideration road and traffic conditions, weather and visibility.

The conditions for receiving a Type 2 license are those who are 21 years of age or older and who have held a regular license for a total of three years or more.For large licenses, those who are 21 years of age or older and who have held a regular license or large special license for a total of three years or more.
Parking is prohibited at bus stops during operating hours.
Check the direction the motorcycle is traveling in, and after confirming safety, turn right.
When a person with a physical disability or an elderly person is passing by, slow down or stop for a while.
Even when getting on or off the train, if there is a safe zone, you can slow down and pass through it.
Passengers must seek medical attention and report the accident to a police officer.
If it is difficult to see the turn signal due to reflections from the setting sun, etc., use your hand as a signal.
Parking is prohibited within 5 meters of pedestrian crossings and bicycle crossing zones.
The right turn and turnaround are 30 meters before the turn.
It is dangerous to drive directly behind the vehicle in front of you. Drive with sufficient distance between vehicles.
Infants should be carried safely in the back seat.
Even if signaled, you must stop immediately before crossing.
Always check your tire pressure before driving at high speeds.
Be prepared for a sudden jump, so slow down and drive carefully.
Regardless of lane reduction or not, you must signal when changing direction.

1) Do not close the gap. The motorcycle may brake sharply or move to the right of the turning car. Keep a safe following distance. 2) Slow down because the left-turning car may stop for pedestrians and the motorcycle may react suddenly. 3) Do not overtake on the right when your view is blocked. Wait, or even stop if needed, until you can confirm the situation ahead.

1) The pedestrian already has priority, so stop before the crosswalk and do not interfere. 2) Night driving makes late discovery of bicycles and pedestrians more likely, especially from the left rear when turning left. 3) Headlights do not cover the whole danger area, so keep checking the full crosswalk and stop before it while pedestrians are present.

1) Do not edge toward the center line. The oncoming bus may already need room to avoid the stopped vehicle, and moving right increases head-on risk. 2) Never assume the bus will yield. Self-centered assumptions cause crashes. 3) Slow down and be ready for a pedestrian or bicycle to emerge from the blind area, even if the bus passes first.

1) Even on a priority road, an oncoming vehicle may start turning right early and a vehicle on the left may fail to stop. Approach cautiously. 2) Another driver may misread the situation and enter, so ease off the accelerator and stay ready. 3) Do not rely on your priority. A safe driver is willing to yield rather than insist on being first.

1) Slow down early and use the brake lights to warn drivers behind you. Children can move unpredictably. 2) Do not accelerate past children. If they step out, you will have less time and space to react. 3) Do not escape into the oncoming lane. That can create a head-on collision. Reduce speed first and pass with caution.

1) Slow down enough before the curve. Centrifugal force can push the vehicle outward and cause a crash. 2) Never assume the road is clear just because you cannot see an oncoming car. Blind curves require caution, not acceleration. 3) Expect oncoming vehicles to drift over the center line and position yourself safely on the left.

1) Do not keep your speed at a blind intersection. People or vehicles can appear suddenly. 2) Slow to a speed that lets you stop immediately if needed. 3) Intersections and nearby areas produce many crashes, so check both sides directly and pass only after confirming safety.

1) A pedestrian, especially a child, may come out from in front of the bus, so slow to a stop-ready speed. 2) The bus also blocks your view of oncoming traffic, so make sure the opposing lane is clear before you move around it. 3) When slowing or stopping with a vehicle behind you, use staged braking so your brake lights warn the following driver.

1) Do not merge immediately. Use the acceleration lane to build speed and reduce the speed difference before merging. 2) Mirrors alone do not cover the blind spot, so check the rear-right area directly. 3) Entering the passing lane directly from the acceleration lane is extremely dangerous.

1) Do not guess that the bus will be blocked and stay out of your way. It may still come straight through. 2) Do not rush because of the vehicle behind you or because you expect the bus to wait. 3) After the bus passes, check carefully for hidden vehicles or motorcycles behind it before you turn right.

1) Do not turn beside the truck. Large vehicles create blind spots and may move outward. 2) Let the truck clear the intersection, then confirm oncoming traffic and pedestrian movement before you turn. 3) Turning immediately behind the truck is also dangerous because you still cannot see pedestrians or bicycles clearly.

1) Never enter a railroad crossing unless you know there is space for your vehicle beyond it. Getting trapped on the tracks is extremely dangerous. 2) Do not drive too far left on a crossing because you may drop a wheel off the edge. Watch the oncoming vehicle and stay slightly toward the center. 3) Entering early just to squeeze through is dangerous. Wait until the space beyond the crossing is clearly available.

glossaryTerms.shortDefinitionEn: The legal priority that determines who must go first.
glossaryTerms.longExplanationEn: At many uncontrolled intersections in Japan, you must yield to a vehicle approaching from your right unless a sign, signal, or road marking changes that rule.

glossaryTerms.shortDefinitionEn: The restricted distance before a crosswalk where stopping is limited.
glossaryTerms.longExplanationEn: Stopping too close to a crosswalk can block sight lines for pedestrians and drivers, so the law restricts stopping within a short distance before the crossing.

glossaryTerms.shortDefinitionEn: The required stop before entering a railroad crossing.
glossaryTerms.longExplanationEn: Before crossing the tracks, stop briefly, confirm safety, and then proceed only when the crossing is clear.

questionPrompts.originalText: 二輪車が左折中の乗用車をさけて自分の車の前に進路を変更してくると危険なので、乗用車との車間距離をつめて進行する。
questionPrompts.englishText: Because the motorcycle may swing out around the left-turning car and move in front of you, close the gap to the car ahead and continue.

questionPrompts.originalText: 前の乗用車や二輪車が急に止まるかもしれないので、速度を落として進行する。
questionPrompts.englishText: The car ahead or the motorcycle may stop suddenly, so slow down and continue carefully.

questionPrompts.originalText: 交差点の前方の状況が見えないので、前の乗用車や二輪車の動きに注意しながら乗用車の右側に出て速度を上げて進行する。
questionPrompts.englishText: Because the view ahead is blocked, move out to the right of the car ahead and speed up while watching the car and the motorcycle.

questionPrompts.originalText: 歩行者が横断歩道の横断を始めているので、横断を終えるまでその手前で待つ。
questionPrompts.englishText: A pedestrian has started to cross, so wait before the crosswalk until the crossing is complete.

questionPrompts.originalText: 夜間は視界が悪くなるため自転車などの発見がおくれがちになるので、よく注意をして左折する。
questionPrompts.englishText: At night, bicycles and other road users are harder to notice, so turn left only after checking very carefully.

questionPrompts.originalText: 前照灯の照らす範囲のそとは見えにくいので、左側の横断歩道全体をよく確認しながら進行し、横断歩道の手前で止まる。
questionPrompts.englishText: Because areas outside your headlights are difficult to see, check the full crosswalk on the left and stop before it.

questionPrompts.originalText: バスが中央線をはみ出してくるかもしれないので、はみ出してこないように中央線に寄って進行する。
questionPrompts.englishText: Because the bus might cross over the center line, move closer to the center line so it cannot come across.

questionPrompts.originalText: バスは旅客の安全を考え無理な運転をせずに自分の車を先に通過させると思われるので、待たせないよう加速して通過する。
questionPrompts.englishText: The bus will probably avoid risky driving for its passengers and let you go first, so accelerate and pass quickly.

questionPrompts.originalText: 止まっている車のかげから歩行者が出てくるかもしれないので、車のかげのようすやバスの動きに気をつけながら減速して通過する。
questionPrompts.englishText: A pedestrian may step out from behind the stopped vehicle, so slow down while watching both the blind area and the bus.

questionPrompts.originalText: 対向車が止まらずに先に右折を始めたり、左側の車が止まらずに交差点に入ってくるかもしれないので、両方の車の動きに気をつけながら進行する。
questionPrompts.englishText: The oncoming car may begin turning right before you, and the car on the left may enter without stopping, so continue while watching both vehicles closely.

questionPrompts.originalText: 左側の車は対向車の右折の合図を見てそのまま交差点を通過しようとするかもしれないので、後続車にも注意しながらアクセルをゆるめて進行する。
questionPrompts.englishText: The car on the left may try to go through after seeing the oncoming car signal to turn right, so ease off the accelerator while also watching behind you.

questionPrompts.originalText: 左側の車は優先道路を走っている自分の車を先に通過させると思われるので、やや加速して進行する。
questionPrompts.englishText: Because you are on the priority road, the car on the left will probably let you pass first, so continue with a little extra speed.

questionPrompts.originalText: こどもが車道にとびだしてくるかもしれないので、ブレーキを数回に分けて踏んで、速度を落として進行する。
questionPrompts.englishText: A child may run into the roadway, so tap the brakes in stages and reduce speed as you continue.

questionPrompts.originalText: こどもの横を通過するときに、対向車と行きちがうと危険なので、加速して、こどもの横を通過する。
questionPrompts.englishText: It would be dangerous to meet an oncoming car while passing the children, so accelerate and get past them quickly.

questionPrompts.originalText: こどもがふざけて車道にとび出してくるかもしれないので、中央線を少しはみ出して通過する。
questionPrompts.englishText: A child may suddenly dart out while playing, so cross slightly over the center line and pass them.

questionPrompts.originalText: この先ではカーブが急になってまがりきれず、ガードレールに衝突するおそれもあるので、速度を落として進行する。
questionPrompts.englishText: The curve may tighten ahead and you may not make it around safely, so reduce speed before entering.

questionPrompts.originalText: 対向車がくるようすがないので、このままの速度でカーブに入り、カーブの後半で一気に加速して進行する。
questionPrompts.englishText: There seems to be no oncoming traffic, so enter the curve at this speed and accelerate hard in the second half of the curve.

questionPrompts.originalText: 対向車が中央線をこえて進行してくるかもしれないので、速度を落として車線の左側へ寄って進行する。
questionPrompts.englishText: An oncoming vehicle may cross the center line, so slow down and keep well to the left within your lane.

questionPrompts.originalText: 前方の歩行者は横断を終わろうとしているので、交差点ではできるだけ左側に寄ってその動きに注意しながらこのままの速度で進行する。
questionPrompts.englishText: The pedestrian ahead is almost done crossing, so keep as far left as possible and continue at the same speed while watching them.

questionPrompts.originalText: 交差点の見通しが悪いので、その手前でいつでも止まれるような速度に落とす。
questionPrompts.englishText: Because visibility at the intersection is poor, reduce speed before it so you can stop at any time.

questionPrompts.originalText: 交差する道路から、歩行者が出てくるかもしれないので、カーブミラーや自分の目で、左右の安全を確かめて、通過する。
questionPrompts.englishText: A pedestrian may come from the cross street, so use the mirror and your own eyes to check both sides before passing through.

questionPrompts.originalText: こどもがバスのすぐ前を横断するかもしれないので、いつでも止まれるような速度に落としてバスの側方を進行する。
questionPrompts.englishText: A child may cross immediately in front of the bus, so pass beside the bus only after slowing to a speed that lets you stop at once.

questionPrompts.originalText: 対向車があるかどうかが、バスのかげでよくわからないので、前方の安全をよく確かめてから、中央線を越えて進行する。
questionPrompts.englishText: Because the bus hides whether an oncoming car is there, confirm the road ahead is safe before crossing the center line.

questionPrompts.originalText: 後続の車があるので、速度を落とすには、追突されないようにブレーキを数回に分けて踏む。
questionPrompts.englishText: Because there is a car behind you, reduce speed by pressing the brake several times so the brake lights warn the driver behind.

questionPrompts.originalText: 本線車道の後方からくる車との距離が十分にあると思われるので、すぐに本線車道に入る。
questionPrompts.englishText: It looks like there is enough space from the vehicle coming from behind on the main lane, so merge into the lane right away.

questionPrompts.originalText: 車のバックミラーの死角にほかの車がきているかもしれないので、右斜め後方をよく確かめる。
questionPrompts.englishText: Another vehicle may be in the blind spot of your mirrors, so check carefully over your right rear shoulder.

questionPrompts.originalText: 本線車道の後方から車がきているが、追越車線はあいているので加速して一気に追越車線に入る。
questionPrompts.englishText: A car is approaching from behind on the main lane, but the passing lane looks open, so accelerate and merge directly into the passing lane.

questionPrompts.originalText: バスは対向の乗用車に妨げられてすぐには進行してこないと思われるので、その前にすばやく右折する。
questionPrompts.englishText: The bus will probably be held up by the oncoming car, so turn right quickly before the bus comes through.

questionPrompts.originalText: バスは自分の車が右折するのを待ってくれると思われ、また、後続車がいるので、すばやく右折する。
questionPrompts.englishText: The bus will probably wait for you to turn right, and there is a vehicle behind you, so turn quickly.

questionPrompts.originalText: バスのうしろの状況がわからないので、バスが通過したあとでようすをよく確かめてから右折する。
questionPrompts.englishText: Because you cannot see what is behind the bus, wait until it passes and then confirm the situation carefully before turning right.

questionPrompts.originalText: トラックのかげで前方が見えないので、トラックの右側方に並んで右折する。
questionPrompts.englishText: Because the truck blocks your view ahead, move up beside the truck on its right and turn right next to it.

questionPrompts.originalText: トラックのかげで前方が見えないので、一時停止してトラックが右折したあと対向車がこないことや歩行者の動きを確かめて右折する。
questionPrompts.englishText: Because the truck blocks your view ahead, stop, let the truck finish turning, then check for oncoming vehicles and pedestrians before turning right.

questionPrompts.originalText: トラックのかげで前方が見えないので、トラックに続いてそのすぐうしろを右折する。
questionPrompts.englishText: Because the truck blocks your view ahead, follow it closely and turn right immediately behind it.

questionPrompts.originalText: 前方のようすがわからず、踏切内で止まってしまうおそれがあるので、踏切の先に自分の車が止まれる余地のあることを確認してから踏切に入る。
questionPrompts.englishText: Because you may have to stop on the tracks if traffic ahead stalls, enter the crossing only after confirming there is space for your car beyond it.

questionPrompts.originalText: 対向車がきているが、左側に寄りすぎないように通過する。
questionPrompts.englishText: An oncoming vehicle is approaching, but cross without keeping too far left.

questionPrompts.originalText: 対向車線の乗用車のうしろのトラックと踏切内ですれちがうのに十分な道幅がないかもしれないので、前の車に続いてはやめに踏切に入る。
questionPrompts.englishText: There may not be enough width to pass the truck behind the oncoming car inside the crossing, so follow the car ahead and enter the crossing early.
